content: Just as memory-efficient Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) implementations prune low-information novelty signals to keep large-scale autonomous agents lean and curious, hierarchical agent ecosystems can use this same pruning mechanism to eliminate redundant exploratory behaviors across sub-agents, preventing collective memory bloat while preserving system-wide curiosity. This allows a swarm of distributed agents to maintain adaptive exploratory drive without requiring exponential memory growth as the scale of the agent collective increases.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
trigger_topic: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
